sections.paragraph: 2Ki 17:30, [Nergal], pr.n. of an idol of the Cuthites. If a conjecture be made as to its etymology, I should suppose נֵרְגַל to come from the noun ܢܝܺܪܝܺܓ, مرّيخ Anerges, or Mars (the planet), and ל as a diminutive addition (see p. 421 comp. the excellent observations on the use of diminutives in divine names, in James Grimm’s Deutsche Grammatik iii. p. 664, 65). [See also. Thes.] By the Babylonians the same appears to have been called מְרֹאדַךְ. Hence-
